title: Fixing Broken HTML Table Structure with Missing Headings

statement.markdown: Given an HTML string representing a table structure, validate that it has at least one table heading (th) element. If the table is empty, return 'Valid' if no heading is present; otherwise, return the index of the first heading found. If the table has more than one heading, return the index of the first heading. If the table has no headings, return '-1'. If the table is empty and has a caption, return 'Valid'.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the HTML table structure. The string may contain one or more tables.
statement.outputFormat: The index of the first th element found, or -1 if no th elements are found. If the table is empty and has a caption, return 'Valid'.
statement.notes: The table structure may contain multiple tables, and the first th element in the first table should be returned.

constraints.description: The length of the input string

examples.explanation: The table has a single heading element at index 0
The table has no heading elements
The table has a single heading element at index 0

hints.text: Start by parsing the HTML string to identify the table structure.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Broken HTML Table Structure with Missing Headings
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem, you need to parse the HTML string and identify the table structure. You can use a library like DOMPurify to parse the HTML.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve parsing the entire HTML string and checking each element to see if it's a th element. However, this would be inefficient and may not be able to handle large inputs.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a library like DOMPurify to parse the HTML string and then use a traversal algorithm to find the first th element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a library like DOMPurify to parse the HTML string and then use a traversal algorithm to find the first th element. You can also handle the case where the table has multiple tables and return the index of the first th element in the first table.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the algorithm correctly finds the first th element in the first table and handles the cases where the table has multiple tables or no th elements.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1. Parse the HTML string using DOMPurify. 2. Use a traversal algorithm to find the first th element in the first table.
commonMistakes: Failing to handle the case where the table has multiple tables
Failing to handle the case where the table has no th elements
